topic: Lightweight 4-row falsifiability module for Overview tab (Item 3 of HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001)
our_argument_summary: EXP-256 commissions a 4-row falsifiability-module on the Overview tab, drawing verbatim from the existing eg-falsifiability section in the Evaluation Guide. The module surfaces the four conditions under which we would upgrade a verdict (discriminating prediction from dome geometry, genuine prospective prediction, derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry, sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs) with current statuses (0/69, 1 partial, 0/69, pending). Pure additive insertion via two patch_segments (HTML + CSS) with verified-unique anchors.

proposal_audit.distillation_faithfulness: PASS - the four module rows are accurate distillations of the eg-falsifiability conditions on docs/index.html lines 543-557. Row 1 captures the 'differs from standard physics + confirmed' pair. Row 2 captures the 'specific/quantitative/station-named/timestamped + anchored independent of observation file' compound. Row 3 captures the Schumann no-globe-radius/no-spherical-eigenvalue criterion. Row 4 captures the no-WGS84/no-curve-fitting criterion. No invented conditions per HNOTE-007 boundary.
proposal_audit.status_grounding: MOSTLY PASS - '0 / 69' for condition 1 is directly grounded ('only 2 of 69 claims produce a quantitative dome-specific derivation, and both fail'). '1 partial - eclipse Aug 12, 2026' for condition 2 is grounded in 'approach this standard but have methodological issues'. '0 / 69' for condition 3 imports a denominator from condition 1 that the source does not explicitly state for the Schumann case (source says 'currently no such derivation exists' without quantification). 'pending - OPEN-001' for condition 4 is grounded in section 1.3 prose and the dome-author's own OPEN-001 entry.
proposal_audit.anchor_uniqueness_FM1: PASS - grep -c on the template-literal-wrapped anchor (with the dollar-brace wrapper) returns 1. Note: grep on the bare call (no template wrapper) returns 2 because the same call appears at line 162 inside an if-block; the proposal's use of the template-literal wrapper makes the anchor unique.
proposal_audit.anchor_uniqueness_FM2: PASS - grep -c on the @media line returns 1.
proposal_audit.line_number_currency: STALE - proposal claims FM1 anchor 'currently sits at ~1364', actual line is 1439. FM2 'currently at line 533', actual is line 691. Anchors still resolve uniquely so no integration impact, but documentation is out of date relative to current generate-html.js.
proposal_audit.css_variable_existence: PASS - every CSS var referenced (--accent, --refuted-solid, --ink-3, --ink-2, --heading, --border, --card-bg, --serif, --sans) is defined in the :root and dark-mode media query at lines 512-513.
proposal_audit.counts_total_in_scope: PASS - counts.total is defined at line 1159 as baseWins.length (= 69 dome-claimed wins) and is in scope at the FM1 insertion point inside the same template literal as the title block. Build-time substitution will work.

hero_composition_review.fold_placement: OK per HNOTE - HNOTE-007 Item 3 explicitly allows 'visible on first load (above the fold or just below the fold)'. Combined hero (title-block ~12rem + thesis-hero ~4rem + bar chart ~16rem + module ~16rem = ~48rem ~ 768px) places the module below the fold on a 1440x900 viewport. EXP-255 thesis-hero is currently flagged for major_rewrite (queue_id 266) due to broken anchors, so the composition this EXP assumes is not yet committable. EXP-256 itself is safe to integrate; the composition argument depends on EXP-255 landing first.
hero_composition_review.composition_with_EXP_255: If EXP-255 lands FIRST and then EXP-256: thesis-hero replaces scorecard, then falsifiability-module inserts between bar chart and the new thesis-hero block. Result: bar-chart -> falsifiability-module -> thesis-hero. Reading order critique: the THESIS sentence should arguably come BEFORE the bar chart and the module (claim -> result -> standard), not AFTER. The proposal's claim that 'either order produces a valid build' is true mechanically but the resulting visual order may not match the operator's stated 'thesis sentence at display scale' intent which implies thesis-first. This is an EXP-255-side concern, not EXP-256-side, but the curmudgeon flagged it during this review for decider awareness.
hero_composition_review.no_redundancy_with_bar_chart: PASS - bar chart shows verdict-distribution (0 confirmed across 6 verdict buckets); module shows criterion-progress on 4 falsification axes. Different questions, different surfaces, complementary not redundant per anticipated_objection #1.

design_rubric_application.keep_adversarial_assessment: Color semantics: fm-status-zero=red(--refuted-solid), fm-status-partial=blue(--accent), fm-status-pending=gray(--ink-3). Hierarchy reads correctly red>blue>gray as severity-of-failure-detection (red=evaluated and 0 met, blue=partial credit per source, gray=dome-acknowledged unsolved). Typography: condition titles 0.95rem sans 600-weight; status-num 0.95rem sans 700-weight tabular; status-sub 0.7rem uppercase tracked. Hierarchy is calm and consistent with surrounding scorecard treatment. Layout: 3-column grid on desktop, 2-column collapse on <=720px; semantic ordering preserved on mobile via grid-template-areas.
design_rubric_application.relax_assessment: Class naming convention (no ds- prefix on new classes) is style-guide territory and does not affect on-page truth parsing - relaxed per rubric but flagged as moderate consistency finding for the decider's call.
design_rubric_application.a11y_carveout: Functional a11y: aria-labelledby + role=status on rows + aria-label with full text on each status. Reasonable. Concern below on contrast measurement.

holes_found.description: WCAG contrast not measured. fm-status-num red (--refuted-solid resolves to #d46a6a in dark mode) on --card-bg (#222240 dark) computes to ~4.43:1 - just below WCAG AA 4.5:1 for normal text (the status-num is 0.95rem ~ 15.2px bold, which falls below the 'large bold' threshold of 14pt = ~18.7px). fm-status-sub gray (--ink-3 #8a8680 in dark mode) on --card-bg #222240 computes to ~4.23:1 - also below AA 4.5:1, and the sub-status is 0.7rem ~ 11.2px which is small text. These are inherited site-wide token issues, not introduced by this EXP, but the EXP adds new usages of both tokens at small sizes. Light-mode contrast is fine (8.78:1 and 5.39:1 respectively).
holes_found.recommendation: Add an explicit acceptance criterion measuring WCAG contrast on dark theme with a screenshot or contrast-checker output. If the inherited tokens fail AA, propose either bumping the status-num to a larger size (1.05rem+) or using a more saturated red token. Acceptable to land with the current tokens and file a follow-up issue for site-wide --ink-3 / dark-mode --refuted-solid review, since these are pre-existing not introduced.

holes_found.description: Asymmetric status framing across conditions 3 and 4 may confuse readers. Condition 3 ('Derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry') shows '0 / 69' in red. Condition 4 ('Sub-5% coordinates without globe inputs') shows 'pending' in gray with 'OPEN-001' sub-status. Both have zero successful demonstrations, but the visual treatment differs sharply. The asymmetry has a defensible source - OPEN-001 is the dome's own explicit acknowledgment of condition 4 as an unsolved problem, while there is no analogous OPEN entry for the Schumann derivation - but a casual reader scanning the module column will see 'why is one red and one gray when neither has been met?' and may not follow the fm-jump link to find out.
holes_found.recommendation: Two options: (a) add a brief footer line under the module explaining the 0/69-vs-pending distinction (e.g., 'Pending = the dome itself flags this as unsolved (OPEN-001); 0/69 = we evaluated all 69 claims and found no derivation.'), or (b) adopt consistent status framing - either both 'pending' (with the dome's framing for condition 4 noted in sub-status) or both '0/69' (with OPEN-001 noted in sub-status for condition 4). Option (a) preserves the asymmetric semantics; option (b) prioritizes visual consistency.

holes_found.description: New CSS classes do not follow the codebase's ds- prefix convention. The codebase uses ds-tab-bar, ds-tab-btn, ds-scorecard, ds-sc-card, ds-verdict-bars, ds-vb-row, ds-breaking-news, ds-stance-statement, ds-section-nav, etc. EXP-256 introduces falsifiability-module, fm-heading, fm-caption, fm-jump, fm-rows, fm-row, fm-condition, fm-desc, fm-status, fm-status-num, fm-status-sub, fm-status-zero, fm-status-partial, fm-status-pending - 13 new classes, none prefixed. The EXP-255 review (queue_id 266) flagged the same convention violation as moderate. Per DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 this is style-guide territory and does not affect on-page truth parsing, so the rubric explicitly relaxes here. Flagged for decider awareness only - recommend integrating either with ds- prefixes added or with a deliberate decision to deprefix all classes in a future cleanup EXP rather than continue the inconsistency.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: rename the new classes to ds-falsifiability-module, ds-fm-row, etc. before integration. Trivial sed pass on the patch text. Or accept the inconsistency and queue a future cleanup EXP.

holes_found.description: Anchor line numbers in the proposal are stale. FM1 'currently sits at ~1364' - actual is line 1439. FM2 'currently at line 533' - actual is line 691 in the current generate-html.js. The anchors themselves are still unique, so integration is unaffected; the documentation is just stale relative to current code.
holes_found.recommendation: Update the anchor_line estimates if the EXP is reissued, or leave as-is and note the current line numbers in the integration commit message.

holes_found.description: Module description for condition 2 uses 'anchored independently from any observation file' while the eg-falsifiability source prose uses 'independently timestamped separately from any observation data'. 'Anchored' is technically more precise (matches OTS terminology used in the Timestamp Error tab), but it introduces jargon a casual reader may not parse without expanding the section. The fm-jump link to eg-falsifiability provides full context.
holes_found.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. If a future revision wants to soften, swap 'anchored' for 'timestamped' to match source prose more literally; this loses precision but gains accessibility.

holes_found.description: Module's '0 / 69' for condition 3 imports a denominator the eg-falsifiability source does not explicitly use for the Schumann case. The source says 'currently no such derivation exists' without quantification. The literal claim '0 of 69 dome WINs derive 7.83 Hz from dome geometry' is true, and the denominator is consistent with condition 1 (which IS explicitly grounded in '2 of 69'), but only one WIN (WIN-001 Schumann) is actually relevant to condition 3 - the other 68 WINs do not even attempt Schumann. A defender might argue '0/69' overstates the scope of evaluation by implying 69 attempts.
holes_found.recommendation: Acceptable as-is for visual consistency with condition 1. If reformulated, '0 attempts succeed' or 'no derivation' would be more precise but breaks the uniform x/69 visual pattern. Curmudgeon preference: keep '0/69' for visual rhythm and rely on fm-jump for nuance.

holes_found.description: Anticipated_objections answers do not address the 'dependence on EXP-255' question raised by the hero composition argument. The proposal claims 'either integration order works' but EXP-255 is currently flagged for major_rewrite (queue_id 266 review found broken find anchors using scorecard/sc-card prefix instead of ds-scorecard/ds-sc-card). If EXP-255 is reissued and the thesis-hero ends up positioned BELOW the bar chart (rather than above), the falsifiability-module sits between bar chart and thesis-hero, which inverts the claim-result-standard reading order the proposal proposes.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should integrate EXP-256 independent of EXP-255 status (the patches do not collide). On the visual reading order: confirm with operator whether thesis-hero should land ABOVE the bar chart (claim first) or BELOW (current proposal). EXP-255 reissue is the right place to settle this; EXP-256 is positionally neutral - it sits below the bar chart in either case.

stronger_arguments.description: The module could include a 5th 'meta' row or footer noting 'Adversarial review accepted: send arguments via the Issue tracker / GitHub' to make the falsifiability commitment bilateral. Currently the module is one-way ('here is what would change OUR mind'); reciprocity ('what would change YOURS') would put the dome author on the same footing.
sources: existing eg-falsifiability prose has the eclipse pre-commitment paragraph that already does this for one specific test

stronger_arguments.description: Condition 3 could be expanded to a 'fundamental + harmonics' criterion (deriving 7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3 Hz simultaneously) rather than just 7.83 Hz. The source prose mentions only 7.83 Hz, but the harmonic structure is part of the same Schumann challenge. This would tighten the criterion substantially since fitting 4 numbers from 1 cavity geometry is much harder than fitting 1.
sources: WIN-001 panel discussion of Schumann modes

kernel_of_truth.description: The module is a genuinely effective falsifiability surface. Persistent overview-level visibility of 'what would change our verdict' disarms the most common rhetorical attack against critical reviews ('you've predetermined the verdict / your standards are unfalsifiable / you'll never accept anything'). The 4-row distillation is the right scope for an above- or just-below-the-fold persistent surface; the fm-jump link preserves access to full prose for readers who want context. Ships independently of the parked EXP-190 Scoreboard work, which is the right call.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel SUPPORTS the module's existence rather than challenging it. Concerns above are implementation refinements (contrast, naming, asymmetric status framing) rather than substance challenges. The proposal's anticipated_objections cover most of the obvious dome-defender attack surface; the gaps are mostly in places where the proposal under-claims rather than over-claims.

citations_verified: eg-falsifiability section exists at docs/index.html line 543, contains the 4 conditions cited by the proposal
OPEN-001 referenced in section 1.3 (line 609) and elsewhere - 'Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 (IN PROGRESS)'
Verdict counts: Refuted 9, Self-Contradicted 14, Misleading 22, Std Model 18, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 - total 71
DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 located at monitor/tinker/operator-directives/ - rubric correctly applied
citations_unchecked: Round-1 reviewer R3 / Round-2 reviewer #3 - claim that this module addresses these specific reviewer recommendations is taken at face value from HNOTE-007
EXP-209 verdict bar chart is referenced as the predecessor block - assumed correct based on file references
Cross-page denominator distinction with section 6.5 prose - not independently verified

deeper_analysis:
EXP-256 is a tightly scoped UX addition that turns existing prose into a structured persistent surface. The substantive truth content is well-grounded: the four conditions are verbatim distillations of the eg-falsifiability prose, the status values are defensible against the source for each condition, and the fm-jump link preserves access to full context. The module is independently shippable from EXP-255 (anchors do not collide) and from EXP-190 (which is parked).

Three concerns merit decider attention before integration:

1. Dark-mode contrast: the inherited --refuted-solid and --ink-3 tokens computed against --card-bg fail or barely-pass WCAG AA at the small font sizes used in the module. This is a site-wide token issue not introduced by this EXP, but the EXP adds new usages worth measuring rather than assuming.

2. Asymmetric status framing: conditions 3 and 4 have zero successful demonstrations but display very differently (red 0/69 vs gray pending). The asymmetry is defensible but may benefit from a one-line footer clarifier or - alternatively - uniform status framing across all unmet conditions.

3. Class naming: the new classes don't follow the codebase's ds- prefix convention. Per DIRECTIVE-20260422-003, this is style-guide territory and does not affect on-page truth parsing, so the rubric relaxes here. Flag for decider's call only.

None of these are blocking. The proposal's anticipated_objections cover the common attack surface; the only objection it under-addresses is the visual-hierarchy dependency on EXP-255 (which is currently flagged for major_rewrite). EXP-256 itself is positionally neutral and integrates cleanly regardless of EXP-255 status.

Under DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 truth-vs-standards rubric, the only KEEP-ADVERSARIAL finding is the WCAG contrast concern (functional a11y). The naming convention finding is explicitly RELAX. The asymmetric status framing is borderline - it could flatten a hierarchy distinction (red vs gray for two unmet conditions), but the underlying semantic distinction (we evaluated vs dome itself acknowledges) is real and the visual treatment reflects that. I'm grading it moderate, not major, because the source prose itself supports the asymmetry (eg-falsifiability does treat condition 4 differently via OPEN-001).
summary_for_decider:
RECOMMENDATION: integrate as-is, OR apply two minor edits before integration:
  - Add WCAG contrast measurement to acceptance criteria (or accept inherited token issue as out-of-scope)
  - Add a one-line footer to disambiguate '0/69' (red) from 'pending' (gray) for unmet conditions

Anchors are correct and unique. CSS variables are all defined. counts.total is in scope. No collision with EXP-255 patches. Patch is purely additive.

Integration order: EXP-256 is independent of EXP-255 status. Recommend integrating EXP-256 without waiting for EXP-255 reissue. The visual hierarchy claim that depends on EXP-255 (thesis-first reading order) is an EXP-255-side concern that should be settled when EXP-255 is reissued.

Verdict shift recommended: NO. The substantive content holds; concerns are implementation refinements.
